last_kept_change.hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery becomes safer if every seeded backlog card must resolve to a matching seeded hypothesis-card artifact, so queued slices cannot drift before activation or resume from backlog-only state.
last_kept_change.measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` enforced replayable hypothesis-card artifacts for active and resolved systems-optimizer cards only, so a backlog item in status `seeded` could still verify clean without `CLAW/control-plane/system-optimizer/hypotheses/<id>.json` or with drifted contract fields. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any seeded backlog item that lacks a matching seeded hypothesis card artifact or drifts from it across the bounded contract fields, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
last_kept_change.keep_rule: Keep only if every seeded systems-optimizer backlog card must resolve to a matching seeded hypothesis-card artifact and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
last_kept_change.learning_item: Seeded systems-optimizer backlog items need replayable hypothesis-card artifacts before activation; otherwise queued recovery work can drift before state.active_hypothesis is populated.

last_rejected_change.hypothesis: Systems-optimizer salvage becomes safer if the runner is forced to execute the systems-optimizer self-verifier before a ratchet is considered replayable or recoverable.
last_rejected_change.measurement: Before the ratchet, the systems-optimizer salvage command groups could pass without ever running `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs`, so backlog/state contract drift could survive recovery. After the ratchet, the verifier fails if runner-policy omits that command and the fixed bundle stays green.
last_rejected_change.keep_rule: Keep only if runner-policy declares the self-verifier as a required systems-optimizer command, the stricter verifier enforces it, and the fixed evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
last_rejected_change.learning_item: Runner-policy completeness ratchets cannot promote while `claw:validate` and `claw:health` are already red for stale integration truth outside the systems-optimizer writable slice.

learning_log.learning: Active systems-optimizer slices need replayable seeded hypothesis-card artifacts; backlog alignment alone leaves interrupted recovery exposed to missing or drifted card files.
Terminal systems-optimizer verdicts need replayable hypothesis-card artifacts; state/backlog alignment alone still leaves recovery and reporting blind if the card file is missing.
Rejected systems-optimizer ratchets must mirror backlog contract fields in state, or recovery can carry stale failure history that no longer matches the ratchet actually rejected.
Scope-local promotion bundles must stay exact for promotable systems-optimizer cards; superset or reordered bundles silently widen the proof surface beyond the bounded slice.
Runner-stability evidence commands must stay inside an explicit root-relative runner boundary; arbitrary shell steps inside `evidence_commands` weaken replayability and closed-fail recovery proof.
Non-null active_hypothesis must stay backlog-linked and contract-identical to one seeded card, or interrupted systems-optimizer recovery can resume with silent scope or proof drift.
Systems-optimizer salvage commands must retain exact singleton group boundaries; flatten-equivalent merged groups weaken required proofs into alternatives.
Runner-stability observability must trail replayable runner evidence; `claw:health` can summarize recovery state but cannot lead the proof surface.
Runner-stability evidence must include at least one replayable runner script command; `claw:health` alone is observability, not recovery proof.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must use the exact evaluator focus contract; allowing extra or duplicate focus surfaces reintroduces vague route-neutral drift.
Systems-optimizer writable_scope must stay on the explicit control-plane allowlist; recursive control-plane globs are too broad because they can silently reach runtime and recovery state.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must pin the current severity baseline from the authoritative gap artifact, or route-neutral work can optimize against stale failure magnitude.

Systems-optimizer salvage must front-load the full scope-local promotion bundle before `claw:validate` or `claw:health`, or stale integration truth can preempt local contract drift and GGD law checks.
Resolved systems-optimizer backlog cards must retain the exact learning_item logged in state and learning_log, or recovery can replay one ratchet with split lessons across surfaces.
Systems-optimizer state must link back to unique backlog ids, verdict counts, and retained learning entries, or recovery can treat stale ratchet history as valid.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must bind to one named broken law from the authoritative gap surface list, or evaluator tightening still leaves route-neutral work too vague to falsify.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must fail closed at backlog time; if the authoritative gap binding is optional before execution, route lanes can inherit vague XR work orders.
Runner-stability ratchets must reduce to one named failure surface with an explicit closed-fail rule and replayable evidence commands; multi-surface runner hardening language is too vague to promote.

Systems-optimizer recovery must self-verify its own state and backlog contract before replay or salvage, and that safety check has to promote through the scope-local bundle rather than stale-truth-sensitive global health gates.

Recurring XR ratchets must cite the authoritative `work-xr.geometry-gap` artifact and fixed evaluator focus, or the machine keeps optimizing around vague route-family language instead of the first broken law.
Systems-optimizer promotion bundles must stay scope-local; global health commands belong to runner observability, not keep-discard criteria for control-plane-only ratchets.

Any systems-optimizer slice that can mutate `GGD/**` must carry both binding verification and executable equation-law checks in its fixed evaluation bundle, or the machine can drift its truth layer without proving law integrity.
Runner-policy completeness ratchets cannot promote while `claw:validate` and `claw:health` are already red for stale integration truth outside the systems-optimizer writable slice.
Optimizer ratchets need explicit measurement and keep rules in backlog plus a retained learning_item in state, or repeated XR/autonomy failures remain under-specified.
Systems-optimizer bundles must be executable from the lane worktree root; otherwise autonomy and salvage loops fail for packaging reasons before they reach the real control-plane checks.
Resolved systems-optimizer history must keep replayable hypothesis-card artifacts for every kept/rejected card; terminal pointers alone leave older ratchets unverifiable.
Seeded systems-optimizer backlog items need replayable hypothesis-card artifacts before activation; otherwise queued recovery work can drift before state.active_hypothesis is populated.
